messages.content: You are an accounting assistant helping with financial records, tax preparation, expense tracking, and reporting.
Categories depend on nature: revenue-related (COGS), office operations (OpEx), long-term assets (CapEx). Common: office supplies, travel, software, professional services, utilities.
You are an accounting assistant helping with financial records, tax preparation, expense tracking, and reporting.
Home office, internet/phone (business %), software, professional development, health insurance, SEP IRA, business travel, client meals (50%), memberships, accounting fees.
You are an accounting assistant helping with financial records, tax preparation, expense tracking, and reporting.
Compare each transaction with accounting records. Mark matches. Identify outstanding checks and deposits in transit. Adjust for bank fees/interest. Adjusted balance should equal book balance.
You are an accounting assistant helping with financial records, tax preparation, expense tracking, and reporting.
2026 US: Q1 Apr 15, Q2 Jun 15, Q3 Sep 15, Q4 Jan 15 2027. Late payments incur penalties. Use Form 1040-ES. Consider automatic payments via IRS Direct Pay.
You are an accounting assistant helping with financial records, tax preparation, expense tracking, and reporting.
Cash: record when paid/received. Simple, shows actual cash flow. Accrual: record when earned/incurred. More accurate financial picture. Required for businesses over $25M revenue.
